text.title: Chunking Semantic — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules: same embedding model as retrieval, threshold within 0.6–0.85 band, max/min size guards, empty-doc short-circuit, strategy version on threshold change.

rule.statement: SemanticChunker MUST use the same embedding model at index time that the retriever uses at query time. The embedding_model name MUST be recorded on every emitted chunk.
rule.rationale: A chunker that uses a different model produces boundaries based on a different similarity signal than the retriever sees — boundaries become semi-random.

rule.statement: similarity_threshold MUST be in [0.6, 0.85] for default English embedding models. Values below 0.6 emit one giant chunk; above 0.85 emit sentence-level fragments.

rationale.source: Threshold-sweep benchmarks on legal + medical corpora

rule.rationale: Outside this band the splitter degenerates; 0.75 is the empirical default that balances coherence and granularity.

rule.statement: Chunks exceeding max_chunk_size MUST be sub-split (recursive splitter); chunks below min_chunk_size MUST be merged into the previous chunk. Both guards MUST be configurable and applied after threshold-based splitting.
rule.rationale: High thresholds on long legal documents emit single multi-thousand-token chunks that exceed embedding caps. Guards prevent the failure mode.

rule.statement: If the document tokenizes to 0 or 1 sentences, return one chunk with the original text and skip the embedding call.
rule.rationale: The naïve loop indexes the embeddings list with `[i+1]` and raises IndexError on single-sentence inputs; the short-circuit also saves a wasted API call.

rule.statement: Changing similarity_threshold OR embedding_model MUST bump strategy_version and write to a versioned collection name.
rule.rationale: Threshold change produces a different chunk distribution; mixing under the same collection breaks A/B comparison.
